title: Youth Shelters - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Youth Shelters 15 Matching Service Providers BOYS & GIRLS AID BOYS & GIRLS AID BEAVERTON TRANSITIONAL LIVING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides temporary housing and support for homeless youth and young adults. The program is designed for young people to work with staff in learning life skills for independent living. Eligibility: Ages 18-24, homeless or a history of housing instability, fleeing domestic violence, low income as defined by federal guidelines Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-5pm, Tuesday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 542-2350 Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS AID BOYS & GIRLS AID HILLSBORO SAFE PLACE FOR YOUTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth shelter. Program provides transportation, basic needs, and case management. Intakes are made available at off-site locations in the community. 454 SE Washington Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Runaway and homeless youth ages 12-20 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 542-2717 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY BASIC CENTER BEND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline, short and long term shelter, case management and advocacy, crisis intervention, short term counseling, and mediation services between youth and their families. 2125 NE Daggett Lane Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Runaway and at-risk youth ages 11-17 Hours: Shelter and Crisis Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Other service times may vary. Email (541) 382-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT LIVING OPTIONS FOR TEENS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides both short term and long term shelter options for youth. Services include housing focused case management, education planning, job and employment search assistance, life skills training, and other supports in transitioning to self-sufficiency. The shelter opens as an emergency shelter as space allows. Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are at risk of homelessness or experiencing housing insecurity Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 660-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHURCH @ THE PARK YOUNG ADULT SHELTER COMMUNITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing with access to case management services. Clients must be able to manage their own activities of daily living, including eating, bathing, and mobility. This program operates on a waitlist. Eligibility: Homeless individuals and couples ages 18-24. One pet per resident is allowed. Hours: Varies Email (971) 375-7491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTEGRAL YOUTH SERVICES MY FRIEND'S PLACE EMERGENCY YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a voluntary short-term shelter that offers a stable home, single rooms, meals, and supportive adults. Must consent to stay and agree to shelter rules, including no weapons or drugs. 505 N 9th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Ages 10-17 experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 884-2319 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES ALBANY HOUSE YOUTH SHELTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency and transitional shelter with access to basic needs (bed, food, shower, laundry, hygiene, clothes, etc.). Length of stay depends on individual needs and engagement in the program. Individuals participate in case management, skill-building, education, mental health supports, and family mediation (if appropriate). Access to activities, mentoring, academic coaches, and employment support. 1240 SE 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: -Ages 10-17 runaway, unaccompanied, or otherwise homeless or at risk -Youth living outside the service area will not be turned away -Must be attending a state-certified education program. If not enrolled in school upon arrival, the agency will support accomplishing this. -Cannot serve youth with a fire-starting history, actively suicidal, or adjudicated sex offenders due to licensing Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 220-2950 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES CORVALLIS HOUSE YOUTH SHELTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency and transitional shelter with access to basic needs (bed, food, shower, laundry, hygiene, clothes, etc.). Length of stay depends on individual needs and engagement in the program. Individuals participate in case management, skill-building, education, mental health supports, and family mediation (if appropriate). Access to activities, mentoring, academic coaches, and employment support. 555 NW Jackson Avenue Corvallis, OR 97339 Eligibility: -Ages 10-17 runaway, unaccompanied, or otherwise homeless or at risk -Youth living outside the service area will not be turned away -Must be attending a state-certified education program. If not enrolled in school upon arrival, the agency will support accomplishing this. -Cannot serve youth with a fire-starting history, actively suicidal, or adjudicated sex offenders due to licensing Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 754-2404 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER PORCH LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides overnight emergency shelter. Youth can access shelter for up to 14 days. Beds are first come, first served. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Access Center: 7 days per week 9am-5pm Shelter: 7 days per week 8:45pm-8:45am Email (503) 380-7200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Access Center is a service for homeless and runaway youth who are seeking intake assessment and referral. Provides basic needs including food, clothing, basic medical supplies, and shelter. Youth receive an assessment to determine if they meet homeless youth services requirements. For youth in crisis, emergency shelter is provided at Porch Light Youth Shelter. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are homeless or at immediate risk of becoming homeless; eligibility is determined through an assessment with the agency Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term overnight shelter, two meals per day, and cleanup facilities for 30 homeless and street dependent youth attempting to make changes in their lives. Youth may access clothing, medical assistance, and employment education support. When severe weather is declared by the city, 5 additional beds will open. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Displaced youth ages 16-24. Youth must be working with a case manager on a plan in order to access a bed on a continuing basis. Hours: 7 days per week 9pm-9am Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 STATION 7 SHELTER AND CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth crisis intervention services and a temporary youth shelter. Services include immediate crisis intervention and counseling, youth and family assessments, and referral assistance. Shelter services offer short-term overnight stays, group counseling, and individual case management. Individuals in crisis may also use the shelter for temporary respite while a long-term plan is developed. 931 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Runaway and homeless youth ages 11-20 -Youth and families in conflict Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 689-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER ALBA COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term emergency shelter, mentorship, family mediation, resource referrals, and support with basic needs, including food, clothing, showers, and hygiene supplies with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 9-17 and experiencing housing instability Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 754-4350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES SAFE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Temporary emergency shelter program for runaway and homeless youth. Works with families and youth to facilitate reunification in a safe/healthy manner. 719 E 1st Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 11-17 who have run away or do not have stable housing Hours: Intake:Monday-Friday 9am-8pm Emergency shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 538-8023 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH EMPOWERMENT SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth in crisis with a place for physical and emotional safety while assisting them in developing life skills that will enable them to make positive contributions in their communities. 610 Court Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Ages 10-19 who are experiencing difficulties at home and have left home or are at risk of leaving and their families. Must not be under the influence of intoxicants and must be nonviolent and cooperative with others in the house. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 769-0262 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS AID SAFE PLACE FOR YOUTH CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EMERGENCY SERVICES HEARTS WITH A MISSION YOUTH SHELTER INTEGRAL YOUTH SERVICES EMERGENCY YOUTH SHELTER JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SHELTERS JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SHELTERS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS PORCH LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 SHELTER AND CRISIS LINE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY HOME YOUTH SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH ALBA COLLABORATIVE YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP SAFE SHELTER YOUTH EMPOWERMENT SHELTER YOUTH EMPOWERMENT SHELTER
